data.title: ICL Group Non-GAAP EPS of $0.09 in-line, revenue of $1.7B; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• ICL Group press release (ICL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.09 in-line.
  • Revenue of $1.7B (+6.3% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $380 million was up 10% versus $347 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • For 2026, the company expects consolidated adjusted EBITDA to be between $1.4 billion to $1.6 billion. For Potash sales volumes, the company expects between 4.5 million and 4.7 million metric tons in 2026.

data.title: SoftBank Group, Toshiba among companies eyeing US-Japan gas project
data.description:
Japanese firms such as SoftBank Group (SFTBY), Toshiba (TOSBF), and Hitachi (HTHIY) (HTHIF) have shown interest in joining a U.S. gas project announced by President Donald Trump, trade minister Ryosei Akazawa said Wednesday.

Other companies have also expressed interest in two additional projects outlined by the U.S. president, Akazawa said.

Mitsui OSK Lines (MSLOY), Nippon Steel (NPSCY), Modec (MDIKF), and JFE Holdings (JFEEF) are interested in providing supplies for a crude oil export facility project, while Asahi Diamond Industrial Co. and Noritake Co. (NITKF) are interested in purchasing from a synthetic industrial diamond manufacturing facility, Akazawa added.

The development comes as Japan intends to invest up to $36 billion in U.S. oil, gas, and critical mineral projects, marking the first portion of its $550 billion pledge under the trade deal reached with President Trump.

“Our MASSIVE Trade Deal with Japan has just launched!” Trump posted Tuesday on social media. “The scale of these projects are so large, and could not be done without one very special word, TARIFFS.”

Most of the first wave of investment will go towards the Portsmouth Powered Land Project, Ohio, which will generate 9.2 gigawatts of electricity each year, according to the administration. It will be operated by SB Energy, which is a subsidiary of Japanese conglomerate SoftBank Group.

data.title: Rush Street Interactive outlines 21%-26% revenue growth target for 2026 as casino-first strategy accelerates user expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rush Street Interactive (RSI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Richard Schwartz, CEO, opened by highlighting a record year, stating, "We continue to set new records in revenue, profitability, cash flow and user counts as well as other core KPIs." He emphasized that 2025 revenue reached $1.13 billion, growing 23% year-over-year and surpassing the high end of raised guidance, driven by broad-based strength across geographies and product lines.
  • Schwartz noted, "Our North American online casino markets continue to drive exceptional growth...with MAUs increasing 51% in the fourth quarter, representing our second highest quarterly growth rate during the past 4.5 years and impressively achieved on a much larger player base and without the benefit of new market launches."
  • The CEO outlined RSI's steadfast focus on a "casino-first strategy" as its key differentiator, with plans to "increase our investments in developing differentiated casino content and online casino legalization efforts" in 2026. He also updated on the company's navigation of Colombia's tax changes, stating, "Despite a temporary drop in net revenue last year, for the full year, we achieved annual GGR growth of 66% and increased MAUs by 34%."
  • Schwartz discussed an upcoming launch in Alberta, describing it as "a significant opportunity for us to leverage our success in other North American online casino markets, particularly given our strong performance in Ontario."
  • CFO Kyle Sauers reported, "Fourth quarter revenue of $324.9 million, up 28% year-over-year, set another record high and marks our 11th consecutive quarter of sequential revenue growth...Fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA of $44.1 million set a new quarterly record and increased 44% year-over-year...Full year adjusted EBITDA reached $153.7 million, an impressive 66% increase year-over-year."

Outlook

  • RSI projects 2026 revenue in the range of $1.375 billion to $1.425 billion, representing 21% to 26% year-over-year growth. Adjusted EBITDA is expected between $210 million and $230 million, a growth of 37% to 50%. Sauers stated, "This new tax environment, combined with the market share gains we achieved in 2025, positions us well for strong growth in Colombia and across Latin America."
  • Management anticipates modest improvement in gross margins and continued marketing spend efficiency, expecting "meaningful increases in marketing spend in 2026 but at a rate slower than our expected revenue growth."
  • The company is not including potential Alberta launch revenue or costs in guidance, highlighting additional upside if the launch proceeds as anticipated.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was $324.9 million, marking an 11th straight quarter of sequential revenue growth. Full year revenue was $1.13 billion, with gross margins of 34.4% for Q4 and 34.6% for the year, consistent with the prior year.
  • Marketing expenses were $45.4 million in Q4 (14% of revenue) and $158.4 million for the year, decreasing as a percentage of revenue. G&A was $22.3 million in Q4 and $81 million for the year, reflecting ongoing investment but also operational leverage.
  • Net income for 2025 was $74 million, compared to $7.2 million in 2024. Cash on hand at year-end was $336 million, with $142 million in cash generated during 2025.
  • MAUs in North America grew 37% year-over-year in Q4 to 278,000, with 51% growth in online casino markets. Latin America MAUs increased 47% to 493,000, despite a challenging tax environment in Colombia.
  • Online casino revenues grew 30% in Q4 and 28% for the year, while sports betting revenue increased 20% in Q4 and 7% for the year. North American revenue rose 29% in Q4 and 25% for the year; Latin America revenue grew 17% in Q4 and 12% for the year.

Q&A

  • Daniel Politzer, JPMorgan: Asked for quantification of Colombia's deposit tax impact and guidance implications. Sauers clarified, "In total, for 2025, we had about $75 million of incremental bonusing that we did due to the VAT tax on the players...probably cost us in the range of $25 million to $30 million in EBITDA on the year."
  • Bernard McTernan, Needham: Inquired about ARPU trends in Latin America. Sauers explained currency fluctuations and noted higher player values in Mexico. McTernan also queried content investment; Schwartz described building an in-house studio and launching proprietary games in 2026.
  • Jordan Bender, Citizens: Questioned minimum bet changes in Illinois and broader tax strategy. Sauers said, "The minimum bet was not necessarily in response to the Chicago tax...we've chosen to use a minimum bet strategy. Could we use that in other markets...absolutely."
  • David Katz, Jefferies: Asked about prediction market products and G&A growth. Schwartz stated RSI is monitoring prediction markets but not prioritizing them. Sauers attributed G&A growth to content and lobbying investments.
  • Ryan Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum: Sought drivers of 51% North American casino MAU growth. Sauers credited efficiency in player acquisition and product quality, with Schwartz emphasizing differentiation and user experience.
  • Other analysts addressed Maine market opportunities, cross-sell from prediction markets, promotional strategies, and poker ecosystem expansion.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive to slightly positive tone, frequently congratulating management and expressing interest in user growth and market expansion. Questions were largely constructive, seeking clarity on tax impacts, content investment, and strategy.
  • Management's tone remained confident and upbeat throughout, especially during prepared remarks, as Schwartz asserted, "We have tremendous confidence in our growth trajectory and strategic positioning."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management sustained an optimistic outlook, but the current quarter featured more detailed discussion of guidance, tax mitigation, and product investment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 was raised, with revenue growth targeted at 21%-26% vs. prior year, compared to Q3's full-year 2025 guidance of $1.1 billion-$1.12 billion in revenue and $147 million-$153 million in adjusted EBITDA.
  • Strategic focus on the casino-first approach was maintained, but the current quarter emphasized increased investment in proprietary content and market expansion, particularly Alberta.
  • Analyst questions shifted from broad market trends and regulatory uncertainty in Q3 to more granular topics such as tax impacts, minimum bet strategies, and marketing efficiency in Q4.
  • Management's confidence remained strong, with a greater focus on operational execution and scalability, while analysts delved deeper into competitive landscape and product differentiation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the continued uncertainty regarding Colombia's tax environment, with Sauers noting, "The Constitutional Court of Colombia suspended the emergency decree...we expect the additional tax to be paid for the month of January."
  • Competitive risks from prediction markets and changes in promotional strategies were discussed, though RSI emphasized its differentiated casino-first approach and disciplined market entry.
  • Analyst concerns centered on the predictability of tax environments, the scalability of recent user growth, and the impact of new product investments on margins.

Final Takeaway

Rush Street Interactive capped a record-setting 2025 with robust revenue, profitability, and user growth across all markets, powered by its casino-first strategy and operational discipline. The company outlined strong 2026 guidance with 21%-26% revenue growth and stepped-up investment in proprietary content and new markets, while navigating regulatory complexities and maintaining a focus on customer experience and long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Mark Zuckerberg set to testify in social media addiction trial
data.description:
Mark Zuckerberg will testify this week in a landmark trial alleging that Meta’s (META) social media platforms deliberately addict and harm children.

The case was brought by a now 20-year-old woman, identified by initials KGM, who claims early social media use worsened her depression and suicidal thoughts. Meta (META) and Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) YouTube are the remaining defendants in the case; TikTok (BDNCE) and Snap (SNAP) have settled.

Zuckerberg, who has previously testified before Congress, will face a jury for the first time on similar youth-safety issues. The trial is one of three bellwether cases that could shape thousands of related lawsuits.

He is expected to face questions about Instagram’s algorithms, infinite scroll features, and appearance-altering filters. Meta is also facing a separate trial in New Mexico that began last week.

A Meta (META) spokesperson said the company strongly disagrees with the allegations in the lawsuit and said they are “confident the evidence will show our longstanding commitment to supporting young people.”

The testimony follows last week’s appearance by Instagram head Adam Mosseri, who rejected claims that social media is clinically addictive and defended the company’s safety efforts.

The Associated Press contributed to this story.

data.title: US agencies develop critical minerals price floor to break China dependence, Bloomberg reports
data.description:
The U.S. has developed a critical minerals price floor system that it’s pitching to allies as the Trump administration and more than 50 countries look to reduce dependence on China for the resources that are deemed critical to national security, Bloomberg News reported.

Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs Jacob Helberg said multiple U.S. agencies have developed the system and are having conversations with allies and partners, the report said. It’s the latest update to progress being made by the US and its allies to ringfence Western companies from China’s pressure on those markets.

“We have had multiple agencies take a close look at this. They have developed a very, very sophisticated price floor system that we are having conversations with our allies and partners about,” Helberg said. “We are very excited about it because pricing is the key to unlocking private investment.”

Industry participants have long considered price floors for critical minerals to protect non-Chinese companies from market flooding by China, which can depress profits for Western firms.

Relevant stocks include USA Rare Earth (USAR), United States Antimony (UAMY), Critical Metals (CRML), Ramaco Resources (METC), NioCorp Developments (NB), MP Materials (MP), TMC the metals company (TMC), Lithium Americas (LAC), Perpetua Resources (PPTA), Nouveau Monde Graphite (NMG), Trilogy Metals (TMQ), Westwater Resources (WWR), Albemarle (ALB), Sigma Lithium (SGML), and Standard Lithium (SLI).

ETFs: (REMX), (XME), (LIT), (BATT)
